messages.text: <@U06F97BPQJU> Here is a one page description of some work we did.  In our first tests Vitala and Vitala plus performed almost as good as boiled linseed oil when used as water repellants.  They were not as good as Thompson's water seal. <https://docs.google.com/document/d/12mrCgvwsXnTlREzyFP4VnhmtPEVtuvHjs-Dvg5FYM5o/edit?usp=sharing>

<@U01LKAFAS58> We want to do some comparison of ESO vs SESO for plasticizing PVC. Would you look and see if we have some PVC? If not, let's order some and once it comes in let's do a basic experiment compounding (process 11) some PVC with ESO and PVC with SESO. Same loading. Get enough to make some of the bigger dog bones, and then do tensile testing to test differences in pliability.

Do you have the bandwidth to look at that sometime this week or next? We're really just looking to see if there is any advantage to using this, and if there is, is it enough to justify getting into that market
I don't have any that I know of. I can make time to do this next week. Will you order?
Let me take a look, there's one place where I might know we have some
Wasn't where I was hoping it was. I've ordered some, it should be here by early next week

<@U01LKAFAS58> There are a ton of papers on PVC plasticizing with ESO. Pick one that seems to be reasonable to determine your dosage and extruder conditions. If you have any troubles finding something you like let me know.
